message:
perf(server): optimize startup latency with deferred backfill, integrity cache, and persistent poller connections

Startup performance:
- Move Tantivy search backfill to a dedicated background thread
  (am-search-backfill) with a configurable delay (AM_STARTUP_SEARCH_
  BACKFILL_DELAY_SECS, default 8s) so the first TUI render is not
  blocked by index synchronization.
- Split readiness_check into readiness_check (full, with integrity) and
  readiness_check_quick (migrations + connectivity only). TUI startup
  and /health endpoint now use the quick variant.
- Add startup integrity fingerprint caching: after a successful
  PRAGMA integrity_check, persist (schema_version, user_version) to
  diagnostics/startup_integrity_cache.json with a 6-hour TTL. Subsequent
  startups skip the expensive check if the fingerprint matches.
- Remove the premature integrity_guard::note_startup_integrity_probe_
  completed() call so the background guard runs its own first cycle.

TUI poller:
- Reuse a persistent PollerConnectionState across poll cycles instead of
  opening a fresh sync connection every interval.
- Use PRAGMA data_version to detect whether the DB has changed since
  the last poll; when unchanged, return a timestamp-refreshed copy of
  the previous snapshot without re-querying all aggregates.
- Replace the sleep-in-small-increments shutdown loop with a
  Condvar-based wait that wakes immediately on stop signal.
- Split ack_pending count into a separate query using inbox_stats
  (with fallback to the original correlated subquery) to avoid slow
  joins on large mailboxes.
- Split reservation fast-path into separate count and snapshot queries
  with a LIMIT cap and ORDER BY expires_ts ASC for deterministic
  ordering. Add released_ts type detection alongside expires_ts for
  correct fast-path eligibility.

Background workers:
- Add graceful startup delays (8-10s) to ack_ttl, cleanup, retention,
  and tool_metrics workers so they do not compete with the initial
  migration and backfill burst. Each uses sleep_with_shutdown() for
  immediate stop responsiveness.

Startup checks:
- Reduce HEALTH_CHECK_TIMEOUT from 2s to 750ms to avoid multi-second
  stalls when probing ports occupied by non-HTTP processes.

TUI app:
- Remove the self-scheduling HousekeepingTick when the deferred action
  drain hits the batch cap; the next frame tick handles it naturally.

TUI bridge:
- Replace thread::yield_now() with a 2ms sleep in the event retry loop
  for more predictable backoff behavior.

Console input:
- Tune INPUT_DRAIN_POLL_TIMEOUT (1ms -> 6ms), INPUT_EMPTY_READY_BACKOFF
  (2ms -> 8ms), INPUT_DRAIN_CAP_STREAK_LIMIT (3 -> 1), and
  INPUT_DRAIN_CAP_BACKOFF (1ms -> 6ms) to reduce CPU spin on idle
  terminals. Remove the yield_now() fallback in the drain cap branch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
